item.title: ECON 1113 – Principles of Economics
item.description:
Semester: Summer 2023

Course Description

This course introduces students to the fundamental concepts of economics, including supply and demand, market structures, and the role of government in a market economy.

Course Overview

In this course, we explored micro and macroeconomic principles through lectures, Canvas discussions, and real-world case studies. Emphasis was placed on developing analytical thinking about economic decisions and policy.

Course Materials

  • [Download Syllabus](/pdf/Syllabus_ECON 1113- 201 Summer 2023.pdf)

Student Evaluation

  • [Course Evaluation](/pdf/CRS Report - ECON 1113-201 - Sara Asgari Gouraj - Summer 2023.pdf)

item.title: ECON 1123 – Principles of Economics
item.description:
Semester: Fall 2023

Course Description

An introductory course covering both microeconomic and macroeconomic principles, tailored for undergraduates from various disciplines.

Course Overview

Through active Canvas engagement, weekly assignments, and concept-based lectures, students gained foundational knowledge in economic reasoning and the functioning of economies at both individual and national levels.

Course Materials

Syllabus not available online.

Student Evaluation

  • [Course Evaluation](/pdf/CRS Report - ECON 1123-851 - Sara Asgari Gouraj - Fall 2023.pdf)

item.title: ECON 2843 – Elements of Statistics
item.description:
Semester: Fall 2024

Course Description

This course introduces statistical tools for analyzing data in economics, including descriptive statistics, probability, and hypothesis testing.

Course Overview

Students engaged with real economic data to build intuition around statistical methods and applied software (Excel/R) to perform data analysis. Weekly problem sets and guided labs supported skill-building.

Course Materials

Syllabus not available online.

Student Evaluation

  • [Course Evaluation](/pdf/CRS Report - ECON 2843-851 - Sara Asgari Gouraj - Fall 2024.pdf)

item.title: ECON 3113 – Intermediate Microeconomic Theory
item.description:
Semester: Summer 2024

Course Description

An intermediate-level course focusing on consumer and producer theory, market equilibrium, and welfare analysis.

Course Overview

We examined theoretical models of individual and firm behavior using algebra and calculus. Emphasis was placed on logical derivation and application of theory to policy and real-world settings.

Course Materials

  • [Download Syllabus](/pdf/Syllabus_ECON 3113- 300 Summer 2024.pdf)

Student Evaluation

  • [Course Evaluation](/pdf/CRS Report - ECON 3113-300 - Sara Asgari Gouraj - Summer 2024.pdf)
